Swoobat

#0527 Woobat #0529 Drilbur
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Swoobat is a Psychic/Flying type Pokémon introduced in Generation 5.

Swoobat

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0528
Type Psychic Flying
Species Courting Pokémon
Height 0.9 m (2′11″)
Weight 10.5 kg (23.1 lbs)
Abilities 1. Unaware
2. Klutz
Simple (hidden ability)
Local № 0034 (Black/White)
0067 (Black 2/White 2)
0056 (X/Y — Coastal Kalos)
0175 (Sword/Shield)
0152 (The Isle of Armor)

Training

EV yield 2 Speed
Catch rate 45 (5.9% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 50 (normal)
Base Exp. 149
Growth Rate Medium Fast

Breeding

Egg Groups Field, Flying
Gender 50% male, 50% female
Egg cycles 15 (3,599–3,855 steps)

Base stats

HP 67
244 338
Attack 57
107 234
Defense 55
103 229
Sp. Atk 77
143 278
Sp. Def 55
103 229
Speed 114
209 359
Total 425 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

Type defenses

The effectiveness of each type on Swoobat.

2½2¼0

Evolution chart

#0527
Woobat
Psychic · Flying
(high Friendship)
#0528
Swoobat
Psychic · Flying

Swoobat changes

  • In Generations 5-7, Swoobat has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Black It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks.
White Anyone who comes into contact with the ultrasonic waves emitted by a courting male experiences a positive mood shift.
Black 2
White 2		 It shakes its tail vigorously when it emits ultrasonic waves strong enough to reduce concrete to rubble.
X
Omega Ruby		 It shakes its tail vigorously when it emits ultrasonic waves strong enough to reduce concrete to rubble.
Y
Alpha Sapphire		 Anyone who comes into contact with the ultrasonic waves emitted by a courting male experiences a positive mood shift.
Sword Emitting powerful sound waves tires it out. Afterward, it won’t be able to fly for a little while.
Shield The auspicious shape of this Pokémon’s nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck.

Moves learned by Swoobat

  • In other generations

Legends: Z-A Sword/Shield

Moves learnt by level up

Swoobat does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Swoobat cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Name origin

swoon
to be seduced
bat
cave-dwelling flying mammal
